title: Casparia latifolia
taxonomy: Casparia latifolia is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Casparia, which is part of the Rubiaceae family that includes over 13,000 species. This genus is primarily found in the tropical and subtropical regions of Asia and Africa. The classification of Casparia latifolia is further supported by its characteristics, such as its woody stems and clusters of small, white flowers. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally considered a member of the Rubiaceae family.
uses: Casparia latifolia is reportedly used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever, cough, and respiratory issues.
